title_variants.title: The Night 5 Planes Vanished Without a Trace
Why the Navy Erased Flight 19's Cause of Death
5 Planes Took Off. None Came Back.

recommended_title: The Night 5 Planes Vanished Without a Trace

description.hook_lines: December 5th, 1945. Five torpedo bombers lift off from Fort Lauderdale on a routine training mission. None of them come back.
description.full_description:
December 5th, 1945. Five torpedo bombers lift off from Fort Lauderdale on a routine training mission. None of them come back.

Flight 19 — fourteen men, five planes — vanished over the Atlantic. Then the rescue plane sent to find them vanished too. Twenty-seven men. Six aircraft. Not a single piece of wreckage ever recovered.

Sources:
- Navy Board of Investigation Report, 1946
- Fort Lauderdale Naval Air Station flight logs

thumbnail_concepts.concept: Split frame: formation of planes on left, empty ocean on right
thumbnail_concepts.rationale: Visual contrast between presence and absence
